items.front: Unless a facility directive authorizes otherwise, what must be used to post current traffic and clearance data?
How must information be entered on en route FAA Form 7230-19 flight progress strips?
On an oceanic ATOP strip, what does the reduced-separation flag "W" indicate?
Who may authorize omission or optional use of certain spaces on terminal flight progress strips?
items.back: The facility manager, in a facility directive, when no misunderstanding will result.

items.front: For an equipment interchange flight, whose company name is used for aircraft identity?
items.back: The operating air carrier's company name.

items.front: What reference governs approved aircraft type designators?
What suffix identifies a solo USAF or USN undergraduate pilot in the flight plan?
items.back: The letter Z; it is not used in ground-to-air communication.

items.front: How should an equipment suffix change be made on supported automation platforms?
items.back: By amending the ICAO CNS equipment codes so automation generates the proper suffix.

items.front: What symbol at the clearance limit shows holding instructions were included in the original clearance?
Can locally approved identifiers be used on teletypewriter or interphone circuits?
items.back: No. They may be used only within your facility.
